mappings.rationaleAI: The East India Company covers its origins in the spice trade, rivalry with the Dutch VOC, territorial acquisition from trading posts to governance, the Battle of Plassey, Clive and Hastings, the Indian Rebellion of 1857, and the transfer of power to the Crown, matching virtually the entire arc of A and B.

The Indian Mutiny of 1857 covers the uprising against Company rule, the siege of Delhi, massacres at Cawnpore and Lucknow, and the British crown's assumption of responsibility for India, directly matching B.3.

The Dutch East India Company covers Dutch trading posts and commercial activities in Asia including rivalry with the British, directly matching A.2 on Dutch trading posts and conflicts with the British.
The Sikh Empire under Ranjit Singh and the Anglo-Sikh Wars leading to British annexation directly match B.1.f on completion of British annexation of the Sikh kingdoms in the 1840s.

The Mughal Empire's decline and the degree to which British colonial administration built upon Mughal precedents directly relate to A.3.b on the transition of Company status, B.2 on political and cultural effects of British influence, and C.1 on the raj.
Annie Besant's involvement in the Indian independence movement and the Home Rule League directly matches C.3 on the beginning of Indian nationalism and C.4 on World War I and the emergence of Gandhi.
The Opium Wars cover the British East India Company's opium trade from India to China, directly relating to British commercial and imperial activities in the Indian subcontinent under A.3.a.
